title: deschampsia cespitosa subsp. beringensis
intro: Deschampsia cespitosa subsp. beringensis, also known as Bering fescue or Bering hair grass, is a subspecies of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is a perennial grass native to North America, known for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant can be prepared in herbal tea or infused oil form, and has been used in traditional medicine to promote relaxation, reduce pain and swelling, and support overall well-being.
